---
title: "MasterControl Performance — Exploratory Data Analysis"
author: "Jeffrey"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
---

# 1. Load required libraries
```{r}
# Load the package

library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(lubridate)
library(tidymodels)
library(vip)
library(corrplot)
library(DALEXtra)
library(finetune)  # For better tuning strategies

# Set seed ins
set.seed(123)
```


# 2. Import and initial data preparation

```{r}
qal_raw <- read_csv("Mastercontrol QAL Performance.csv")

qal <- qal_raw |>
  clean_names()

glimpse(qal)
```

# 3. Target construction

```{r}
qal <- qal |>
  mutate(
    progressed = case_when(
      next_stage_c %in% c("SQL", "SQO", "Won") ~ 1,
      next_stage_c %in% c("Recycled", "Lost", "MQL", "INQ") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

qal_model <- qal |>
  filter(!next_stage_c %in% c("Exclude")) |>
  filter(!is.na(progressed)) |>
  mutate(
    progressed = factor(progressed, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("no", "yes"))
  )

qal_model |>
  count(progressed) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n))
```
# 4. Feature engineering

```{r}
qal_model <- qal_model |>
  mutate(
    title_lower = tolower(contact_lead_title),
    seniority = case_when(
      str_detect(title_lower, "chief|ceo|cfo|coo|cio|cto") ~ "C-Level",
      str_detect(title_lower, "vp|vice") ~ "VP",
      str_detect(title_lower, "director") ~ "Director",
      str_detect(title_lower, "manager") ~ "Manager",
      is.na(title_lower) ~ "Missing",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    has_quality = str_detect(title_lower, "quality"),
    has_ops     = str_detect(title_lower, "operat|manufactur|engineer"),
    has_reg     = str_detect(title_lower, "regulat")
  )
```

# 5. Train/Test Split
```{r}

qal_split <- initial_split(qal_model, prop = 0.8, strata = progressed)

qal_train <- training(qal_split)
qal_test <- testing(qal_split)

# Cross-validation folds (5-fold, stratified)
set.seed(123)
qal_folds <- vfold_cv(qal_train, v = 5, strata = progressed)
```


# 6. Enhanced recipe with better preprocessing

```{r}
qal_recipe <- recipe(progressed ~ 
                      # Account attributes
                      acct_primary_site_function +
                      acct_manufacturing_model +
                      acct_target_industry +
                      acct_territory_rollup +
                      acct_tier_rollup +
                      
                      # Contact attributes
                      seniority +
                      has_quality +
                      has_ops +
                      has_reg +
                      
                      # Additional enhanced features
                  
                      last_tactic_campaign_channel,
                    data = qal_train) %>%
  
  # Handle missing values with indicators
  step_indicate_na(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  
  # Convert logicals to factors
  step_mutate(
    has_quality = factor(has_quality, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE)),
    has_ops = factor(has_ops, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE)),
    has_reg = factor(has_reg, levels = c(FALSE, TRUE))
  ) %>%
  
  # Handle unseen levels
  step_novel(all_nominal_predictors(), -all_outcomes()) %>%
  
  # Replace NA with "unknown"
  step_unknown(all_nominal_predictors(), -all_outcomes()) %>%
  
  # Collapse rare categories
  step_other(all_nominal_predictors(), -all_outcomes(), threshold = 0.05) %>%
  
  # Create dummy variables
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors(), -all_outcomes()) %>%
  
  # Remove zero variance predictors
  step_zv(all_predictors()) %>%
  
  # Impute remaining NAs in numeric columns
  step_impute_median(all_numeric_predictors()) %>%
  
  # Normalize numeric predictors for models that benefit
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

# Check the recipe
qal_recipe
```
# 7. Model specifications

```{r}
# 7.1 Logistic Regression 
glm_spec <- logistic_reg() %>%
  set_engine("glm")

# 7.2 LASSO (with tuning)
lasso_spec <- logistic_reg(
  penalty = tune(),
  mixture = 1
) %>%
  set_engine("glmnet")

# 7.3 Decision Tree
tree_spec <- decision_tree(
  cost_complexity = tune(),
  tree_depth = tune(),
  min_n = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("rpart") %>%
  set_mode("classification")

# 7.4 Random Forest
rf_spec <- rand_forest(
  mtry = tune(),
  trees = 100,
  min_n = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("ranger", importance = "permutation") %>%
  set_mode("classification")

# 7.5 Gradient Boosting (XGBoost)
xgb_spec <- boost_tree(
  mtry = tune(),
  trees = 100,
  tree_depth = tune(),
  learn_rate = tune(),
  loss_reduction = tune(),
  min_n = tune(),
  sample_size = tune()
) %>%
  set_engine("xgboost") %>%
  set_mode("classification")
```

# 8. Workflow 
```{r}
glm_wf <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(qal_recipe) %>% add_model(glm_spec)
lasso_wf <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(qal_recipe) %>% add_model(lasso_spec)
tree_wf <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(qal_recipe) %>% add_model(tree_spec)
rf_wf <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(qal_recipe) %>% add_model(rf_spec)
xgb_wf <- workflow() %>% add_recipe(qal_recipe) %>% add_model(xgb_spec)
```

# 9. Hyperparameter grids
```{r}
# LASSO grid
lasso_grid <- grid_regular(
  penalty(range = c(-4, -0.5)),
  levels = 15
)

# Tree grid
tree_grid <- grid_regular(
  cost_complexity(),
  tree_depth(range = c(3, 10)),
  min_n(),
  levels = 4
)

# Get number of predictors for mtry range
prepped_recipe <- prep(qal_recipe, training = qal_train)
n_pred <- ncol(juice(prepped_recipe)) - 1  # subtract outcome column

# Random Forest grid
rf_grid <- grid_regular(
  mtry(range = c(2, min(20, n_pred))),
  min_n(range = c(2, 20)),
  levels = 5
)

# XGBoost grid
set.seed(123)
xgb_grid <- grid_latin_hypercube(
  mtry(range = c(2, min(20, n_pred))),
  tree_depth(range = c(2, 8)),
  learn_rate(range = c(-3, -0.5)),
  loss_reduction(range = c(-5, 1)),
  min_n(range = c(2, 20)),
  sample_size = sample_prop(range = c(0.5, 1)),
  size = 20
)

```

# 10. Model tuning
```{r}
# Define metrics
metrics <- metric_set(roc_auc, pr_auc, accuracy, sens, spec)

# Control for tuning
ctrl <- control_grid(
  save_pred = TRUE,
  verbose = TRUE,
  allow_par = TRUE,
  parallel_over = "everything"
)

# Fit baseline GLM
glm_res <- glm_wf %>%
  fit_resamples(
    resamples = qal_folds,
    metrics = metrics,
    control = control_resamples(save_pred = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)
  )

# Tune LASSO
set.seed(123)
lasso_res <- lasso_wf %>%
  tune_grid(
    resamples = qal_folds,
    grid = lasso_grid,
    metrics = metrics,
    control = ctrl
  )

# Tune Decision Tree
set.seed(123)
tree_res <- tree_wf %>%
  tune_grid(
    resamples = qal_folds,
    grid = tree_grid,
    metrics = metrics,
    control = ctrl
  )

# Tune Random Forest
set.seed(123)
rf_res <- rf_wf %>%
  tune_grid(
    resamples = qal_folds,
    grid = rf_grid,
    metrics = metrics,
    control = ctrl
  )

# Tune XGBoost 
set.seed(123)
xgb_res <- xgb_wf %>%
  tune_grid(
    resamples = qal_folds,
    grid = xgb_grid,
    metrics = metrics,
    control = ctrl
  )

```
# 11. Model comparison
```{r}
# Collect results
glm_metrics <- collect_metrics(glm_res) %>% mutate(model = "GLM")
lasso_metrics <- collect_metrics(lasso_res) %>% mutate(model = "LASSO")
tree_metrics <- collect_metrics(tree_res) %>% mutate(model = "Decision Tree")
rf_metrics <- collect_metrics(rf_res) %>% mutate(model = "Random Forest")
xgb_metrics <- collect_metrics(xgb_res) %>% mutate(model = "XGBoost")

# Combine metrics
all_metrics <- bind_rows(
  glm_metrics,
  lasso_metrics %>% filter(.metric %in% c("roc_auc", "pr_auc", "accuracy")),
  tree_metrics %>% filter(.metric %in% c("roc_auc", "pr_auc", "accuracy")),
  rf_metrics %>% filter(.metric %in% c("roc_auc", "pr_auc", "accuracy")),
  xgb_metrics %>% filter(.metric %in% c("roc_auc", "pr_auc", "accuracy"))
)

# Best models by AUC
best_models <- all_metrics %>%
  filter(.metric == "roc_auc") %>%
  arrange(desc(mean))

print("Model Comparison (ROC AUC):")
print(best_models)
```
# 12. Select best model and finalize

```{r}
# Select best model based on ROC AUC
best_model_name <- best_models$model[1]
best_model_res <- switch(best_model_name,
                         "LASSO" = lasso_res,
                         "GLM" = glm_res,
                         "Decision Tree" = tree_res,
                         "Random Forest" = rf_res,
                         "XGBoost" = xgb_res)

# Get best hyperparameters
if(best_model_name != "GLM") {
  best_params <- select_best(best_model_res, metric = "roc_auc")
  final_wf <- finalize_workflow(
    switch(best_model_name,
           "LASSO" = lasso_wf,
           "Decision Tree" = tree_wf,
           "Random Forest" = rf_wf,
           "XGBoost" = xgb_wf),
    best_params
  )
} else {
  final_wf <- glm_wf
}

# Final fit on test data
final_fit <- last_fit(final_wf, qal_split, metrics = metrics)

# Test performance
test_performance <- collect_metrics(final_fit)
print("Final Model Performance on Test Set:")
print(test_performance)

```

# 13. Feature importance for best model
```{r}
final_model <- extract_workflow(final_fit)

if(best_model_name %in% c("LASSO", "GLM")) {
  # For linear models, extract coefficients
  if(best_model_name == "LASSO" && exists("best_params")) {
    library(glmnet)
    
    coefs <- extract_fit_parsnip(final_model)$fit %>%
      coef(s = best_params$penalty) %>%
      as.matrix() %>%
      as.data.frame() %>%
      rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
      rename(coefficient = s1) %>%
      filter(coefficient != 0) %>%
      arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))
    
    print("Top 10 LASSO Coefficients (Non-zero):")
    print(head(coefs, 10))
  } else if(best_model_name == "GLM") {
    coefs <- extract_fit_parsnip(final_model)$fit %>%
      summary() %>%
      coefficients() %>%
      as.data.frame() %>%
      rownames_to_column("feature") %>%
      filter(`Pr(>|z|)` < 0.05) %>%  # Only significant at p < 0.05
      arrange(desc(abs(Estimate))) %>%
      head(10)
    
    print("Top 10 Significant GLM Coefficients:")
    print(coefs)
  }
  
} else if(best_model_name %in% c("Random Forest", "XGBoost")) {
  importance <- final_model %>%
    extract_fit_parsnip() %>%
    vip::vi() %>%
    arrange(desc(Importance))
  
  print("Top 10 Feature Importance:")
  print(head(importance, 10))
}
```
# 14. Model diagnostics

```{r}
# Predictions on test set
test_preds <- collect_predictions(final_fit)

# ROC Curve
roc_curve <- test_preds %>%
  roc_curve(truth = progressed, .pred_yes) %>%
  autoplot() +
  labs(
    title = paste("ROC Curve -", best_model_name),
    subtitle = paste("AUC =", round(test_performance %>% 
                                      filter(.metric == "roc_auc") %>% 
                                      pull(.estimate), 3))
  )

print(roc_curve)

# Confusion Matrix
conf_mat <- test_preds %>%
  conf_mat(truth = progressed, estimate = .pred_class)

print("Confusion Matrix:")
print(conf_mat)
```
# 15. Business insights
```{r}
# Progression by seniority
seniority_analysis <- qal_test %>%
  bind_cols(
    predict(extract_workflow(final_fit), new_data = ., type = "prob") %>%
      rename(pred_prob_yes = .pred_yes)
  ) %>%
  group_by(seniority) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    actual_rate = mean(progressed == "yes"),
    predicted_rate = mean(pred_prob_yes),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(actual_rate))

print("Progression by Seniority Level:")
print(seniority_analysis)

# Impact of functional indicators
functional_impact <- qal_test %>%
  bind_cols(
    predict(extract_workflow(final_fit), new_data = ., type = "prob") %>%
      rename(pred_prob_yes = .pred_yes)
  ) %>%
  summarise(
    quality_yes = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_quality == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE),
    quality_no = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_quality == FALSE], na.rm = TRUE),
    quality_impact = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_quality == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE) - 
                     mean(pred_prob_yes[has_quality == FALSE], na.rm = TRUE),
    
    ops_yes = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_ops == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE),
    ops_no = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_ops == FALSE], na.rm = TRUE),
    ops_impact = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_ops == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE) - 
                 mean(pred_prob_yes[has_ops == FALSE], na.rm = TRUE),
    
    reg_yes = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_reg == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE),
    reg_no = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_reg == FALSE], na.rm = TRUE),
    reg_impact = mean(pred_prob_yes[has_reg == TRUE], na.rm = TRUE) - 
                 mean(pred_prob_yes[has_reg == FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
  )

print("Impact of Functional Indicators on Progression:")
print(functional_impact %>% select(contains("impact")))
```
